channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /GRT/ The Graph — 28 replies — 36.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
/GRT/ The Graph

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-04-20 12:08 UTC

OP

Is it truly over GRTbros?

Will our GRTies ever come back from obscurity? Or is it truly lost?

Early replies (first 3)

62128193
>>62128177
MY GRT GRT GRT MAKES MY FELLOW TRANNIES SQUIRT SQUIRT SQUIRT

62128195
>>62128193
...we deserve the hell we're in

62128200
>>62128195
100% Bitcoin you nigger.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62128202
>>62128200
>100% Bitcoin
That's a surefire way to remain a poorfag

A well placed altcoin bet will always outperform bitcoin

62128208
>>62128202
...like GRT? you dumb fuck

62128224
>>62128208
>...like GRT?
Sure why the fuck not

62128247
It's over wasn't just a meme. If you didn't sell in 2021, you're a retard.

62128257
>>62128247
I did sell in 2021

I'm just thinking it's about time to jump back in

It's probably gonna start rallying up against Bitcoin now

62128260
>>62128177
There is no difference between this baggie and a million other different alt coin baggies. Most alts are going to zero. Sorry not sorry.

62128262
>>62128260
Nah, GRT is built different

62128269
>>62128262
Yes and another 2021 rally is totally coming back again for digital nothing pixels. What makes your ponzi any different from the next ? Kek Baggies.

62128282
>>62128269
I guess we'll just have to wait and see who ends up on top

In about a year GRT will reach 2021 highs and I will be laffin'

62128644
GRT is going to $9.99. GRT, AMP and JASMY are Kek's chosen coins.

62128731
>>62128247
Too red eye, bless up rassclat. Yuns nuh wut da papa be singing! Ya no call dem ‘gaters long mouths till ya pass dem. We booyaka Babylon in due tim mon. Big up ya self natty dreads, when chicken marry, hawk dey near. But I be a broken record. They ai dat web3 wooooooo, faze me. Jah guide.

62129571
>>62128731
Rasta anon is back!

62129610
Sold at a loss for a tax write off.
Silver linings and all that.

62129655
>>62128177
Shit's dead. Turns out the Google of blockchains was the AskJeeves of blockchains. So pissed at myself for buying the top at 37 cents.

62129743
>>62129655
>Shit's dead
For now, but we're probably at the generational bottom right now
It's the time to slurp up cheapies

62129969
No one uses graph databases anymore. Why would anyone use one with a blockchain attached?

62130358
>>62129969
>Midwit take
TheGraph is not a graph database

62130457
>>62129610
I'd start to buy back in if I were you

62131285
It's been over for years

62133372
>>62130358
Dem bullheads tink a chart is a graph. Bun weed. Everything is Everthing is everywhere all da tim as one. Jah bless.

62133566
>>62131285
Maybe it's been so long that it's unovered over and now it's no longer over?

You ever thought 'bout that? Huh?

62133917
>>62133372
>>62128731
Sold everything. Enough is enough.

62133964
The Scam

62134608
>>62133917
If rasta anon makes you sell, you were ngmi in the first place

62134612
>>62133964
It's not just any scam
It's the Duck Duck Go of scams!

item.title: Investment forecasting: 2028 US Elections — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Investment forecasting: 2028 US Elections

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-20 12:43 UTC

OP

Current forecasting suggests that Gavin Newsom will run for president in 2028 and, given the current state of the Republican Party, that he will likely win. Running with that assumption, what would be the best investments to make now beforehand?

I’m very bullish on carbon credit markets.

Early replies (first 3)

62134469
>>62134463
I plan to vote Democrat for the first time in my life in November. Enough is enough.

62134471
how to profit from carbon credit markets? i looked into ctx for investing and/or trading but they need a high payment to onboard and the price of the credits is not really going up.

62134487
>>62134471
Compliance credits. KRBN ETF holds California, EU, RGGI, and UK carbon allowance futures.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134494
>>62134463
>skip ad

62134496
>>62134463
It depends I could see people not voting for him just because of how much of a shithole California is in. I hear there's homeless people everywhere and everything in expensive asf

62134497
>>62134463
Everyone hates Californians and Newsom has too many skeletons in his closet

62134529
>>62134463
AOC is the next president if Trump doesn't rig a third term. The best investment is to have a secondary passport. Preferably to a 3rd world shithole with high food production and clean water.

62134537
>>62134529
USA already rejected a white and brown woman, it’s over xister.

62134697
I'm thinking of buying a bush block, 12 acres of land full of trees and a creek running through it. Hopefully having land with trees will give me some benefits when carbon credits come in.

item.title: >Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA >USSR collapses >Japanese economy soars, expected to overtake USA >Japan… — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA >USSR collapses >Japanese economy soars, expected to overtake USA >Japan…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-04-20 12:54 UTC

OP

>Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA
>USSR collapses
>Japanese economy soars, expected to overtake USA
>Japan's economy completely tanks and they still haven't recovered
>EU overtakes USA for a short while, but starts to stagnate
>Chinese economy soars, expected to overtake USA
>China starts to stagnate while USA continues its steady growth
Is competing with America just a death wish economically? Why can't anyone win against the USA?

Early replies (first 3)

62134540
>>62134386
>Why can't anyone win against the USA?
Inevitably you end up with the US being your biggest trade partner, if you economically start eclipsing them it's ironically bad for business.

And that includes the USSR.

62134577
>>62134386
>Soviet economy soars, expected to overtake USA
LOL that never happened. The USSR was a train wreck from the very start. Stalin managed to force industrialization on what had been an agrarian peasant society, but their factories were complete garbage and their productivity was always shit.

>Japan
>China
Both of them engaged in bubble financing and beggar-thy-neighbor mercantilism to try to pwn the U.S. Both of them ran out of money before they could wreck enough U.S. industries to take over.

>EU stagnates
They did it to themselves with all their political policies, ranging from "green energy" to "let's shit on white males and drive them out of society" to "hey we don't have enough workers so let's import illiterate stupid monkeys".

62134579
>>62134386
Literally nothing I own was made in America

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134700
Fox on the business board.

62134702
>>62134386
A: The Federal Reserve Banking Cartel.

62134711
>>62134700
Averi mating press.

62134718
>>62134700
what an oddly familiar looking vixen

62134723
>>62134577
Correct. They never teach you this in the USA but 200,000 Americans went to the Soviet Union along with hundreds of thousands of Westerners to help build it. They believed in socialism.

Other Western specialists like factory engineers and such were hired out by American companies to build Soviet factories.

Russia without non-Russian tech and specialists is an impossible shithole.
Even post 1990 who built the Russian oil and gas infrastructure?
German engineers, companies, and specialists.

item.title: >new version of Brave called Origin where people pay $60 to remove BAT from the browser — 7 replies — 23.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>new version of Brave called Origin where people pay $60 to remove BAT from the browser

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-04-20 12:36 UTC

OP

lmao thank god i sold this shit

Early replies (first 3)

62133733
>>62133602
You're joking right? I've been holding 100k BAT since May 2017.

62133734
>>62133602
can we sue brendan eich for false advertisement and stealing our hard earned money for his bullshit shitcoin?

62134088
>>62133602
wait what, are you joking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134105
Holy shit, glad I got rid of this shit back in 2019 and never looked back.

62134245
>>62133733
>>62133733
>>62134088
>>62134105
>What is Brave Origin?
Brave Origin is a paid* version of the browser for users who don’t need all the features that support Brave as a business, but still want the privacy that only Brave offers. Origin users will continue to benefit from our industry-leading privacy, adblock, and speed (via Shields), as well as regular software updates, Chromium patches, and security and privacy improvements. Origin will remove** most other features from the browser.
https://support.brave.app/hc/en-us/articles/38561489788173-What-is-Brave-Origin
Its not for the sole purpose of removing BAT.

62134316
>>62134245
>Its not for the sole purpose of removing BAT.
>Look, we know we have a lot of absolutely shit features that absolutely nobody wants. Rather than basing our business around selling features that people DO want, we'll base it around charging to get the trash removed.
I'm not sure your clarification helped in any single way.

62134683
>>62133734
this fuck brave fuck bat

item.title: HAPPENING — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-20 12:52 UTC

OP

AI BOOM POISED TO BE ‘MASSIVELY DISINFLATIONARY’, NORTHERN TRUST SAYS

Early replies (first 3)

62134662
mumus just making shit up now

62134674
>>62134636
>YOU WILL HAVE UNIVERSAL HIGH INCOME
>YOU WILL OWN A PENTHOUSE
>AND YOU WILL BE HAPPY
All you have to do is let the AI overlords take over. Seems like a no-brainer to me

62134676
>>62134662
bobos just waiting to buy at triple the current price in october

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134721
deflation is bad (but so is inflation and crabflation)

item.title: Will AMC give me generational wealth — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Will AMC give me generational wealth

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-04-20 12:47 UTC

OP

Will AMC give me generational wealth

Early replies (first 3)

62134685
no.

62134694
AMC bagholder general when?

62134699
>>62134694
they must have all killed themselves by now surely.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62134705
>>62134680
generational debt more like it

item.title: When the recession hits and mass layoffs occur, more people will have to start paying their FUTA — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
When the recession hits and mass layoffs occur, more people will have to start paying their FUTA

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-20 12:47 UTC

OP

When the recession hits and mass layoffs occur, more people will have to start paying their FUTA

Early replies (first 3)

62133727
hope it's not too big

62133729
>>62133727
$7000 a year

62133732
>>62133725
Damn reminded me of futanari expansion, those Japanese are master economists

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62133738
>>62133729
>>62133727
>>62133725
Does this count for them not being enrolled in insurance. We should double all of this

62133765
>>62133725
>Futa tax
I love this crazy world kek

62134470
>>62133725
Looking forward to getting absolutely FUCKED by futa

62134551
>>62133729
>have to pay $7000 to stay unemployed
land of the free

62134558
>>62134551
it's paid by employers, not employees.

62134706
>>62133729
It cannot be more than $42 per employee

>>62134558
Correct.

t. Actually own a biz.

item.title: Living with your parents — 41 replies — 17.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Living with your parents

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-04-20 10:29 UTC

OP

Is this the ultimate /biz/ hack? I'm investing 50+% of my GROSS income

Early replies (first 3)

62133220
>>62133182
In most the world it's normal.
In America it was not normal since boomers could bug house with one month of work but the standard they put out is fastly eroding as housing is increasingly becoming unattainable with no relief in sight

62133227
>>62133220
not everyone's job/career is within driving distance/train/whatever of their parents house.

people move for work.

62133257
>>62133220
I could easily afford rent (good job relative to living in flyover country) but just seems better to invest that instead
>>62133227
That's fair but I'd think it would actually be easier in cities than it is for me

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62133410
>>62133182
not paying rent while you work full time is like a cheat code for acquiring wealth quickly as a wagie.

62133421
>>62133410
other than a career at a big globohomo cooperation its basically the only way for most people

62133422
its literally what you're supposed to be doing if you dont want to be a wageslave

62133710
>>62133182
I'm 28 and doing this, but my parents are starting to get angsty

62133719
>>62133710
that’s ok, if you’ve been doing it right you should have enough money for a down payment on a house now.

62133742
Ultimate biz hack is Debtmaxxing+credit card fraud+not paying rent (squatting)

62133749
>>62133182
>Finish 6th form
>Get job because university is a scam
>Hey anon, now you're working, we're going to charge you housekeeping
>End up paying the same as if I'd moved out
>34yo and still stuck at home with mum as moving out would now make her homeless
Fuck this gay earth

62133752
>>62133182
investing into what. memes? index funds?
the more ridiculous these memes get the better

62133767
>>62133182
i invest 70% of gross and live alone dont be so poor

62133772
>>62133182
It makes you go insane. I couldn't stand living with my retarded boomer parents.

62133789
>>62133182
>be me
>live with my mom
>started investing 1k/month
>halfway to five figs
>support my family by paying her rent instead of the landjew
>get homecooked meals everyday
Honestly living with your parents is the ultimate life hack.
Don't move out at 18
Stack and be happy until you've atleast hit 6 Digits.

62133806
>>62133719
I have $324k. I've looked at houses but I always get outbid.

62133809
>>62133772
This.
Today I had a fight with my dad after he threatened to drive drunk (again). I even had to restrain him.
It's financially better (duh, of course) but it destroys you slowly.

62133827
>>62133182
My parents kicked me out when I was 19 but I’ve since forgave them. Today I have children of my own and I wouldn’t dare to do that to them. This topic comes up a lot with other parents my age and we all come to the same conclusion. The world is vastly different than it was in the 1990s and we have to do our this topic comes up a lot with other parents my age best to set them up right. In some ways Generation X had it easy.

62133828
>>62133827
>we have to do our best.

Sorry typo!

62133836
>>62133809
Your dad sounds based

No jk, he sounds like my dad, last time I brought my friend over and he totally freaked the fuck out over nothing and started yelling at me

Like bro I’m 29 years old, I’m just gonna go ahead and never talk to you again if you’re gonna be a fag

62133840
>>62133806
give em nominal monthly payment otherwise they will resent.

62133857
>>62133182
I make 10k a month and pay $500 for rent living with a coworker

62133876
>>62133836
I know you're joking, but there's nothing based about an alcoholic dad that is ruining other people's lives

Alcohol is really the ultimate goyslop. Poison for the soul.

62133879
>>62133182
If I wasn't living with my parents for years, I couldn't afford to buy a house in 2020 and would have been priced out.

62133938
>>62133857
How?

62133951
Stunted growth doesn't pay off in the long run..

62133961
>>62133876
My dad is also an alcoholic faggot

Millions of dollars networth and sits around hungover all the time in a filthy house

Fuck him

62134000
>>62133772
Same, of course as soon as I moved to a different city my dad starts blowing up my phone. Nah, nigga. You're getting 2 phone calls a year. I did everything I was asked for 20 years, got a decent degree on time, got a solid job at 22, and you still give me this shit. Just because I graduated during covid and had most of my plans scrapped for something that wasn't my fault. What a fucking faggot, I hate people like this

62134011
>>62133182
That's how immigrants move up IN AMERICA.

62134029
>>62133182
Depends. I'm 29 and still live with my parents; I just put a house under contract. If you're mortgaging a house it's going towards your equity so it's not like you're throwing it down a black hole. And some houses in my area have increased 100% within the past year. The house I'm buying last sold in 1998 for 35k and I offered 235k for it. The housing market is fucking stupid. So yeah, I wouldn't have made it this far (70th percentile net worth for my age) if I didn't bum it at my parents for long as I did and instead became a rentoid right after college.

On the other hand your social life get's knee capped HORRIBLEY living with your parents past the age of 22. I think I missed out on a lot. Inviting girls over to try and fuck them or inviting your bros over to have some beers and talk shit is out of the question. You can't do that with your parents in the background. Or idk maybe it's normal for thirdies to do it >>62133220. But it'd be beyond embarrassing for me.

Honestly, fuck boomers for making housing so unaffordable where the average joe can't move out by late teens/early 20s. Just another example of the elderly acting as vampire against the youth

62134300
>>62133182
>Is this the ultimate /biz/ hack?
Yes, but normies will mock you for it.
>>62133951
>Stunted growth doesn't pay off in the long run..
There's an argument to be made for this, but women are all chasing the top % of men; if that's not you, then you'll be living somewhere in a more expensive situation, continuing to watch women all chase the top % of men. If you're a pretty average guy and mildly spergy, then it's worth optimizing to make money.

62134440
>>62133182
It's the most OP strat, even if you're flipping burgers, you can retire in 20 years. Boomers don't even realize that it is possible.

62134447
>>62133182
Yes I live with my parents because it doesn't make sense to do otherwise for me right now. They're fine with it since they lived with their parents for a long time and I sometimes borrow them money when they need it so they don't have to pay interests to banks. In the mean time I pay almost nothing in terms of living costs and they'll remember me better than my sister when it comes to the will.

62134451
>>62133182
>>62133772
There's a reason why rent is so expensive. Imagine having to answer where/why you're going out at night, or being asked to do the dishes/take the trash out at 30 years old.

item.title: CLEAN IT UP WAGIE CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP — 69 replies — 13.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
CLEAN IT UP WAGIE CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-04-20 07:37 UTC

OP

CLEAN IT UP WAGIE

CHOP CHOP HURRY UP OR GET WRITTEN UP

Early replies (first 3)

62119436
>>62119165
legend

62119443
Some people have almost no power over anything in their life. They do trivial evil acts like this because its their only thing they can do. Stealing stationary from work is a similar thing. His life is probably miserable.

62119490
>>62119443
Put the fries in the bag, wagie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62127189
>>62119165
n word behavior honestly

62127194
this thread is retarded

62128020
>>62127194
Hurry up with my order, wagie.

62128023
>>62128020
>>62124173
>>62123792
infini necrobump glownigger retard thread
look at the timestamps

62128026
i already told you i don't have a job
if this weren't a retard thread you'd have come up with something funnier but it's blatant vapid spam
this site is fucked

62128031
avoiding flamewarring with me to keep the post count lower & thereby keep the thread up for longer
like i said this site is fucked, everyone is way too self-aware and there are shills around every corner and the mod team treats it like a top-down rat maze with no engagement

62128032
>>62123557
here's another one

62128038
>>62119165

62128047
i'm tempted to just fill the thread with replies & see what happens

62128050
it would take hours & there'd likely be duplicate spam threads on the board within 48 hours though
makes you wonder what the mods are doing, or at least it should
>>62123557
>>62119165

62128085
>>62119443
kek

62128168
>>62128038
if you'd done a shitty mspaint trace job it would still have a soul, like pic related

62128292
>>62119165
>be me
>restaurant wagie in singapoor
>some mutt tourist think its funny to throw ketchup on the ground
>find mop and tell him to clean up
>hahaha no you are slave you do it
>call police
>he starts going off about first amendment and rights
>police gets agitatef because he doesnt do as they tell him, he argues like typical american
>they grab him to take into police car
>he starts fighting back, yelling about rights
>they taze him and beat him with police sticks
>take him to jail
I was kekking the entire time

62128310
>>62127025
Target and Trader Joe’s are can’t miss.

62130589
>>62125676
How did she manage to smuggle all that onto the flight?

62130590
>>62128292
Cope wagie. You still had to clean it up.

62130710
This thread reminded me that Kek Adams has passed.

62130818
>>62130710
Guy was such a betamale

62132756
>>62130818
Well duh, working in an office is the hallmark of betas everywhere.

62132767
>>62132756
He drew comics.
Well, you either work in an office or youre some low iq blue collar.

62132860
trickle down (onto the floor) economics.

62133126
>>62130710
>>62130818
>>62132756
weak niggas still crying about the man lmao

62133190
>>62119165
lmao americans are actually so cucked
if a customer does stupid shit we throw him out and tell him to stfu haha

62133420
>>62130710
rip

62133812
>>62128292

He was just trying to enjoy a succulent Chinese meal

62133862
>>62128038
This is an accurate depiction of the person in the image. Some people are so embarrassingly pathetic then act like they're some edgy Nihilist. You've only got this one lifetime to repent your wickedness before you meet God

62133918
Why is being in the service industry so bad in America? Like something about it is just so demeaning and people are so shitty and rude.

Being a waiter in some small village in europe would prob be chill and relaxed.

62134155
>>62119443
this, except i have complete control over my life and do it out of malice/mischief

62134162
>>62130589
You are allowed to bring any non-liquid foods onto planes.

62134229
>>62125676
What a nightmare

item.title: How much BTC you holding right now? — 91 replies — 10.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much BTC you holding right now?

Open thread • Replies: 91 • Updated: 2026-04-20 04:10 UTC

OP

How much BTC do you currently have in your wallet, and what’s your target amount to hold before it reaches $1M?

>I mean, if you don’t even have at least 1 BTC and you’re in crypto, it still feels pointless.

Early replies (first 3)

62114988
>>62114930
1.03

ill keep buying as pay cheques come in as long as we stay around the 4 year SMA

ill stop buying when we start running up and instead start selling to buy other assets, probably pay off my mortgage

62115186
>>62114930
i cope with my 5 stack and tell myself in 10 years i'll make it.

62115188
>>62114930
350 BTC or so.

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62123856
>>62115773
Not enough

62123911
>>62121496
Just sell it today bobo

62124101
>>62114930
I hold 0.84 btc at Kraken

62124449
>>62123590
You have a point here tho. The use cases are still early, so skepticism makes sense. What’s interesting is the idea of extending utility while keeping Bitcoin unchanged and kept in self custody.

62124459
>>62116911
>ETH is cool but why LINK?

62124487
2.3 BTC and 36 ETH.

62124590
>>62115225
Giga Chad, I kneel

62124643
>>62117007
His choice lol

62125521
>>62117185
$30k? Let's see if you're right.

62125560
1.4 BTC

It doesn't feel like enough. I'm 36 now, and my dream of living a wealthy and comfy life before the age of 40 is basically done for. Is it even possible to have a fun life at 50+? I really lament not getting into BTC back in 2014. It's not like I didn't know about it back then.

62126507
Is BTC even worth having anymore? Seems to have had pretty shit returns since the covid boom.

62126533
>>62114930
https://timesofmalta.com/article/elusive-bitcoin-founder-adam-back-lives-malta.1126648
What are the financial implications of this?

62126561
>>62123814
What risk are you talking about here? Also, it’s better now that there are returns instead of just holding with no yield. This setup is way better overall.

62126610
>>62117395
but why did you sell?

62126617
>>62117832
buy the bottom? but at which price?

62127919
>>62124487
More!!!

62128353
>>62120218
YOU CAN DO IT BOI

62128654
>>62125560
Don't worry about it. Keep going and have fun. YOLO.

62130077
>>62122141
Why FUD

62130099
0.3

62131443
>>62130099
Just 0.3? Make it whole bitcoin

62131447
>>62126507
since the COVID boom? It was just $8k during covid

62131531
>>62114930
The new paradigm is no more peaks.

62131569
>>62114930
Zero and zero

62132043
1.72 bitcoins
*sigh*
I keep stacking, maybe one day, I'll have 2 whole bitcoins.

62132222
around 10, i might end up keeping them forever as a backup plan. Looking at my taxes my average accumulation price was 29k and sell price was 109k Tidy little 70k each. I'll buy back in when the dump is over and it settles between 20-30k. for supposedly being a crypto board you all sure seem to struggle with basic pattern recognition, this is exactly what happens every time, dump 75% from the peak and then the next bull run starts.

62132243
>>62115773

62132565

62133350

item.title: Let's All Get Rich Together — 28 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Let's All Get Rich Together

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-04-20 09:10 UTC

OP

>Gold: World's biggest asset, now worth $33 trillion
>Bitcoin: Was in the top 10 of all assets, peak of $2.5 trillion

And yet nothing happened.

Why?

Because neither of these assets threaten the dollar, or any fiat currency (total market cap of all currencies is about twice that of gold now at its peak)

The plan: We all buy and hold nano (XNO), causing the price to rise and associated interest to grow, with a push for adoption as a currency.

>Why not [my shitcoin] instead of nano?
Nano is the apex currency, both in performance (feeless; fastest settlement ~350ms; lowest energy cost per transaction) and ideologically (fully distributed by the founder for free; no inflation).

It has been around for over a decade and only improved over time, a functional, decentralised, cryptocurrency. If you don't believe me, ask an AI model.

>Won't we get dumped on by each other?
People will sell, but so long as the idea survives, the potential marketcap is still deep in the trillions.

Nothing is more powerful than an idea.

Early replies (first 3)

62118570
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

62118587
Wait a minute ... those are the exact same arguments ...

are you saying that ... ?

no ...

let the goyim deal in the real world

item.title: Something is coming soon — 26 replies — 4.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Something is coming soon

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-04-20 07:15 UTC

OP

If you’re wondering where the recent FUD is coming from it’s because we’re only months away from the release of the CyberCode 2.0 testnet. As an insider, you have no idea what the importance of CbC 2.0 is going to be for LINK when the alpha releases around the end of November (give or take a few months) so I will spoonfeed you out of kindness of heart:

CyberCode 2.0 secures the ledger cryptographically to encode transactions at 20x of the legacy infrastructure. It achieves this by taking blockchain transactions to the BbC-2026 layer and parsing them through the node network incentivized by super linear sequencing (SLSeq) which Sergey said is very close to the finish line. This is all dynamically incentivized through semi-permeable staking (SPS) allowing LINK token holders to benefit for verifying data both on the main chain and the Blockchain Byzantine Consensus 2026 layer. No other project is even close to doing this in the oracle space rn. It’s not even close!

The obvious benefits of super fast transaction speeds (obSFTS) also brings exponential economies of scale. What this means is if farmer has 50 Kg pig for sale, he can put the beast to market on the blockchain to verify the weight in a trust less system faster than global SWIFT system. Soon any business who wants to stay relevant will have to buy LINK to have access to the CyberCode 2.0 feature. A truly unique proposition! Those who don’t use this system (powered by LINK) will get left behind. So get your ducks in a row. Just keep buying and NEVER sell. We are all in this together LINKbloodbros.

Scammer motherfuckers want you to think that banks and other organizations are not currently fulling their bags with cheap LINK as we speak. DON’T NOT LET THEM WIN! If you hold 50 or more LINK right now, you already made it. Immediately when we breach the $10 resistance it’ll be too late for you. You have been warned. So go buy many LINK for you dream to come true.

Early replies (first 3)

62125665
>>62125647
DR;NS

62125781

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless???

62132856
>>62132686

62133186
>>62132552

Most expensive card is the neo discovery holo Umbreon.

I hate the Umbreon you posted, no idea why it has so much hype..

62133365
